summary.what_we_were_doing: Evolved wispr-flow-dark-smokey from a fragile v1.1.0 dark mode script into a production-quality v1.3.0 tool, with three expert agent audits (bash, CSS, Electron), then began scoping a broader context-warming/verification system and Bear note capture pipeline.
key_decisions: CSS uses filter inversion (invert .85, hue-rotate 180, sepia .30, brightness .95) — only durable approach for CSS Modules apps with hashed class names
Modal overlays targeted by exact CSS Module hashes (.mgB3HwW13t29DKsEkfUD, .Lo3Dvv9YLP6w7ogTpGvR, .h4ZXMTnO1FZBj82_Jjao) — break on app updates, documented with grep command to find new ones
asar upgraded to @electron/asar@4.2.0, backup now includes .unpacked/ dir for native binaries
Script renamed from wispr-dark-mode to wispr-flow-dark-smokey — all naming synced (display: 'Wispr Flow Dark-Smokey', code: 'wispr-flow-dark-smokey')
Idempotent strip-and-repatch pattern replaces skip-if-patched — allows CSS updates without full restore cycle
Three expert agents dispatched in parallel (bash/CLI, CSS/UI, Electron) — user expects this pattern for all multi-domain work
current_blockers: UNCOMMITTED: v1.3.0 script, CLAUDE.md, README.md not committed or pushed to GitHub — must do this first
UNCOMMITTED: Open security PR #1 on GitHub (mktemp for temp files) — already incorporated in v1.3.0, PR can be closed
CSS TWEAK REQUESTED: User wants slightly darker, a few percent less sepia, slightly cooler white balance — adjust before committing

BEAR SKILL NOT BUILT: Research done (API docs scraped, grizzly CLI installed, openclaw/bear-notes skill installed) but proper Bear note creation skill with templates, image workflow, and correct URL encoding not yet built
CONTEXT WARMING: Core foundational problem identified but not solved — user cannot verify what context actually loaded at session start vs what Claude is faking
TERMINAL READABILITY: User frustrated with flat monochrome terminal output — wants visual hierarchy, color, possibly TUI-level formatting
next_steps: 1. Tweak CSS: slightly darker (maybe invert .87), less sepia (maybe .22-.25), cooler white balance
2. Commit and push v1.3.0 to GitHub (script + CLAUDE.md + README.md), install to ~/.local/bin/
3. Close PR #1 on GitHub (security fix already in v1.3.0)
4. NEW SESSION: Context warming and verification system — the Memento problem. How to make session bootstrap reliable and provable.

5. NEW SESSION: Bear note capture pipeline — global skill with proper templates (CommonMark, not markdown images), grizzly CLI or correct x-callback-url encoding (%20 not +), image attachment via /add-file endpoint, web clipping via /grab-url
6. NEW SESSION: Terminal output readability — investigate outputStyle setting, statusLine customization, TUI libraries (charsm, ink), markdown formatting discipline
7. Explore ~/Claude/Github/darkreader as reference for CSS injection techniques
files_touched: CLAUDE.md (new — project docs with naming, patterns, gotchas)
README.md (updated — synced naming, v1.2.0 badges, dark dialog feature)
~/.local/bin/wispr-flow-dark-smokey (installed — but may be behind repo version)
summary.important_context: User (Simon) runs a complex AI tech stack: Claude Code (max plan), Obsidian vault (second brain), Bear app (curation intake), episodic memory plugin, mem0 databases, 52+ cloned GitHub repos. The CORE problem is not any single tool but context continuity — every new session starts amnesiac. User is frustrated by: (1) guessing instead of researching (ALWAYS use context7 + WebSearch + Exa + firecrawl before acting), (2) not dispatching domain-expert agents in parallel, (3) lazy/cheap tool usage (user pays for max plan, use it), (4) flat terminal output readability, (5) running ahead without alignment. Hardware: Intel i7-3615QM (2012 MacBook Pro) — NO Bun, NO AVX2. Bear notes MUST have: strict templates, images/screenshots, verbose detail, visual quality. Bear API: use %20 not + for spaces, images via /add-file not inline markdown, /grab-url for web clipping.
